title: linum nervosum subsp. nervosum
intro: Linum nervosum subsp. nervosum, also known as the Narrow-leaved Flax or Wild Flax, is a perennial plant species of the flax genus. It is a flowering plant with medicinal properties, primarily used for its seeds which are rich in mucilage and fatty acids. The health benefits of Linum nervosum subsp. nervosum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues. The plant is often prepared as a tea, oil, or tincture for various herbal remedies, and its seeds can be used to make linseed oil, which is used in cosmetics and pharmaceuticals.
